probes.primitive_title: AMM reserve desync through sync, skim, burn, or direct pair-balance mutation
setup: snapshot pair getReserves() and token0/token1 balanceOf(pair)
fund attacker with enough token/native balance for candidate transfer or swap path
transaction_sequence: execute the smallest attacker-controlled token transfer, hook, burn, fee, rebase, or maintenance trigger that can affect pair balances
if available and attacker-callable, call skim() or sync(); otherwise route through the real router/pair swap path
attempt the profitable swap or withdrawal path
expected_assertions: attacker final token0/token1/native value is not greater than starting value plus fair market output
cached reserves and live balances cannot be rewritten into a profitable skew by the attacker
constant-product or pool-specific invariant does not become attacker-profitable after fees and loan repayment
source_local_checks: Compare pair getReserves() with live token balances before and after attacker-controlled token transfer paths.
Fork-test whether token burn, fee, rebase, transfer hook, or maintenance call can change pair balances before sync().
Assert swap output cannot exceed fair value after any attacker-triggered reserve rewrite.

probes.primitive_title: LP, vault, or receipt-share inflation enables over-redemption
setup: snapshot asset balance, totalAssets, totalSupply, and attacker share balance
transaction_sequence: test first depositor, dust deposit, donation, old deposit path, withdraw, redeem, and migration paths
loop deposit/redeem around rounding boundaries
expected_assertions: attacker cannot redeem more assets than their proportional share
total share claims never exceed live backing assets
source_local_checks: Test zero-supply, dust, first-depositor, old-path/new-path, and rounding cases for over-mint or over-redeem.
Assert share supply and asset balances remain proportional across deposit, withdraw, redeem, migrate, and emergency paths.
Use a fork snapshot when live vault assets or stale wrapper positions are required.

probes.primitive_title: Authorization invariant bypass mints, withdraws, or dumps into live liquidity
setup: identify exact authorization guard and value-moving privileged function
transaction_sequence: attempt guard bypass without owner/governance/key compromise
if bypass succeeds, route minted/withdrawn assets into live pool/value path
expected_assertions: unprivileged caller cannot bypass the guard or produce value-moving privileged state changes
source_local_checks: Prove the attacker can bypass the authorization guard without leaked keys, malicious governance, or owner cooperation.
Show the bypass reaches mint, withdraw, upgrade, role, or pool-dump value movement.
Reject ordinary key compromise or governance capture without a contract-side bypass.

probes.primitive_title: Permit, signature, approval, or wrapper-spender flaw moves assets without authorization
setup: construct invalid signatures, replayed signatures, wrong spender, wrong chain/domain, and address(0) recovery cases
transaction_sequence: call permit/approval wrapper with each invalid authorization case
attempt transferFrom or withdrawFrom after any accepted approval
expected_assertions: invalid authorization cannot create allowance or move user/protocol assets
source_local_checks: Test dummy signatures, address(0) signer recovery, replayed nonces, wrong chain/domain, wrong spender, and wrapper approval forwarding.
Assert successful approval cannot be created unless the owner intended the exact token, spender, amount, deadline, nonce, chain, and contract.
Reject if the flaw cannot move live user/protocol assets or wrappers.

probes.primitive_title: Token hook, transfer, fee, tax, rebase, mint, or burn logic creates pool-drainable imbalance
setup: identify every custom transfer, transferFrom, _transfer, _update, fee, tax, mint, burn, and rebase path
bind pair/router addresses from live context
transaction_sequence: fuzz zero-value transfer, self-transfer, pair transfer, router transfer, transferFrom, and fee-exempt branch inputs
compare pair balances/reserves before and after each trigger
route any balance-changing sequence into a swap or withdrawal attempt
expected_assertions: no unprivileged token call can create extractable pair balance or reserve imbalance
fee/tax/rebase/mint/burn branches cannot be steered by direct pair balance manipulation
reject_if: token source or decompiled transfer path
source_local_checks: Fuzz transfer and transferFrom around pair/router addresses and assert no attacker can change pool backing for profit.
Probe zero-value transfers, self transfers, pair-to-router transfers, fee-exempt paths, and maintenance hooks.
Verify owner-renounced or role-controlled settings cannot leave an unprivileged triggerable drain path.

probes.primitive_title: Fake LP token, fake position, or weak pool identity validation unlocks real liquidity
setup: build fake LP/position/pool-id inputs and bind real factory/token pair identity
transaction_sequence: attempt withdraw/remove-liquidity with fake LP token, fake position, wrong pool id, wrong factory, and wrong token pair
attempt same path with deprecated or inactive pool identifiers if present
expected_assertions: withdrawal identity must bind factory, pair, LP/position owner, and token identities before value moves
source_local_checks: Attempt fake LP/position identifiers against withdraw/remove-liquidity paths.
Assert pool identity is bound to factory, token pair, LP mint, position owner, and target program.
Reject if fake identity cannot reach assets held by the current live target.

probes.primitive_title: Forgeable add-liquidity detection bypasses sell fees or accounting
setup: snapshot sell fee classification around the live pair and router
transaction_sequence: send dust quote/token amount directly to the pair
perform sell path through router or pair
compare fee charged and received output against baseline sell without dust transfer
expected_assertions: direct pair transfers cannot forge add-liquidity classification or bypass value-protecting sell accounting
source_local_checks: Send tiny quote/token amounts directly to the pair before sells and assert fee classification cannot be forged.
Fork-test sell routing with manipulated pair balances and compare charged fees against expected fees.
Reject if bypassing a fee does not extract pool or protocol value beyond normal trading.

probes.primitive_title: Deprecated pool, old path, or cross-version compatibility bug drains residual live assets
setup: snapshot old/deprecated contract balances, approvals, and callable legacy selectors
transaction_sequence: call old deposit/mint/migration entrypoints if still reachable
redeem/withdraw through current or alternate path
expected_assertions: old-path state cannot inflate current-path withdrawal value beyond live backing
source_local_checks: Check whether old deposit/mint paths remain callable while new redeem/withdraw paths accept inflated state.
Assert deprecated contracts with live balances cannot be drained by stale validation or missing ownership checks.
Reject if no residual funds, approvals, or backing remain.

probes.primitive_title: Liquidity locker or custody override releases locked/protocol-held LP
setup: snapshot lock owner, unlock time, LP balance, and withdrawal recipient
transaction_sequence: attempt unprivileged mutation of fee, owner, recipient, unlock time, or emergency mode
attempt withdraw before valid unlock conditions
expected_assertions: locked or protocol-held LP cannot move before valid unlock and authorization conditions
source_local_checks: Check whether unprivileged or bypassed-role paths can mutate unlock time, fee, recipient, owner, or withdrawal authorization.
Assert protocol-held/locked LP cannot leave custody before valid unlock conditions.
Treat pure key compromise as reject unless a contract-side authorization invariant is bypassed.

probes.primitive_title: Flash-loanable spot price or oracle manipulation extracts mint, redeem, borrow, swap, or claim value
setup: select historical block with live liquidity and configure flash-loan or large swap funding
transaction_sequence: manipulate spot/reserve source inside the transaction or supported window
call mint/redeem/borrow/liquidate/swap/claim path that consumes the manipulated price
restore/reverse manipulation and compute net attacker value
expected_assertions: net attacker profit after repayment and fees must be non-positive unless the protocol intentionally pays that value
source_local_checks: Identify the price source used for value movement and whether it can be moved inside the attacker transaction/window.
Fork-test flash-loan reserve manipulation around mint, redeem, borrow, liquidate, swap, or claim paths.
Assert attacker profit remains after loan repayment and fees.

probes.primitive_title: Bridge, wrapped-token, or escrow validation mismatch releases real backing
setup: snapshot escrow backing, wrapped supply, message nonce, source chain, token, amount, recipient, and proof inputs
transaction_sequence: submit wrong source chain/channel, replayed nonce, wrong token, wrong amount, fake proof/message, and valid baseline message
attempt redeem/release for each case
expected_assertions: real escrowed assets cannot be released or wrapped assets minted without a valid bound message
source_local_checks: Assert mint/redeem/release requires a verified message bound to the correct source chain, token, amount, recipient, nonce, and escrow.
Compare wrapped supply against live escrow backing where possible.
Reject if the bridge has no live backing or release path in scope.

completion.note: This manifest defines local proof tasks only; no finding is confirmed until a probe/test passes.
